sentences.sentence: The Painting Fool is one of a growing number of computer programs which, so their makers claim, possess creative talents.
sentences.translation: “绘画傻瓜”是越来越多据其开发者声称拥有创造才能的计算机程序之一。

structure.text: is one of a growing number of computer programs
which, so their makers claim, possess creative talents

difficult_points: one of 后接复数名词 computer programs，表示“许多程序中的一个”。
which 引导定语从句修饰 computer programs，其中 so their makers claim 是插入语。
possess creative talents 是开发者声称的内容，作者语气并未完全背书。

sentences.sentence: Classical music by an artificial composer has had audiences enraptured, and even tricked them into believing a human was behind the score.
sentences.translation: 由人工作曲家创作的古典音乐让观众陶醉，甚至让他们误以为乐谱背后是一位人类作曲家。

structure.text: Classical music by an artificial composer
and even tricked them into believing a human was behind the score
believing a human was behind the score

difficult_points: has had audiences enraptured 是“have + 宾语 + 宾补”结构，表示让观众处于陶醉状态。
trick them into believing 表示“诱使他们相信”，into 后接动名词。
a human was behind the score 是 believing 的宾语从句，指人们以为乐谱由人创作。

sentences.sentence: Artworks painted by a robot have sold for thousands of dollars and been hung in prestigious galleries.
sentences.translation: 由机器人绘制的艺术作品已卖出数千美元，并被挂在知名画廊中展出。

structure.text: have sold for thousands of dollars
and been hung in prestigious galleries

difficult_points: painted by a robot 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 Artworks。
have sold 和 been hung 共用助动词 have，构成两个现在完成时谓语。
been hung 是被动语态，说明作品被画廊悬挂展示。

sentences.sentence: And software has been built which creates art that could not have been imagined by the programmer.
sentences.translation: 还有人构建了能够创作出程序员本人也无法想象的艺术的软件。

structure.text: which creates art that could not have been imagined by the programmer
that could not have been imagined by the programmer

difficult_points: has been built 是现在完成时被动语态，强调软件已经被开发出来。
which 引导定语从句修饰 software，that 引导定语从句修饰 art。
could not have been imagined by the programmer 表示连程序员原本也无法预想。

sentences.sentence: Human beings are the only species to perform sophisticated creative acts regularly.
sentences.translation: 人类是唯一会经常进行复杂创造性行为的物种。

structure.text: the only species to perform sophisticated creative acts regularly
to perform sophisticated creative acts regularly

difficult_points: the only species 后接不定式，说明这个物种具有什么独特能力。
regularly 修饰 perform，强调复杂创造性行为在人类中是持续发生的。

sentences.sentence: If we can break this process down into computer code, where does that leave human creativity?
sentences.translation: 如果我们能把这一过程分解成计算机代码，那人类创造力又处于什么位置呢？

structure.text: If we can break this process down into computer code
we can break this process down into computer code
where does that leave human creativity

difficult_points: If 从句提出假设：创造过程能够被转化为代码。
break...down into... 表示把复杂过程分解成更小单位。
where does that leave... 是反问式表达，询问某事对人类创造力地位的影响。

sentences.sentence: 'This is a question at the very core of humanity,' says Geraint Wiggins, a computational creativity researcher at Goldsmiths, University of London.
sentences.translation: 伦敦大学金史密斯学院的计算创造力研究者杰兰特·威金斯说：“这是一个触及人性核心的问题。”

structure.text: This is a question at the very core of humanity
a computational creativity researcher at Goldsmiths, University of London

difficult_points: 引语在前，says Geraint Wiggins 是后置引述结构。
a computational creativity researcher... 是同位语，补充说明 Geraint Wiggins 的身份。
at the very core of humanity 强调问题触及人类身份的根本。

sentences.sentence: 'It scares a lot of people.
sentences.translation: “这让很多人感到害怕。
difficult_points: It 指代上一句中人工创造力对人类身份提出的问题。
scares 表示这种问题引发的是情绪上的不安或恐惧。

sentences.sentence: They are worried that it is taking something special away from what it means to be human.'
sentences.translation: 他们担心它正在从“成为人意味着什么”之中拿走某种特别的东西。”

structure.text: that it is taking something special away from what it means to be human
what it means to be human

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，说明 people 担心的具体内容。
take something away from... 表示从某个概念或价值中剥离东西。
what it means to be human 是名词性从句，表示“为人意味着什么”。

expressions.text: what it means to be human

sentences.sentence: To some extent, we are all familiar with computerised art.
sentences.translation: 在某种程度上，我们都熟悉计算机化艺术。
difficult_points: To some extent 限定判断范围，避免说法过于绝对。
be familiar with 是固定搭配，表示“熟悉”。

sentences.sentence: The question is: where does the work of the artist stop and the creativity of the computer begin?
sentences.translation: 问题是：艺术家的工作在哪里停止，而计算机的创造力又从哪里开始？

structure.text: where does the work of the artist stop
and the creativity of the computer begin

difficult_points: 冒号后是 the question 的具体内容，以直接疑问句形式呈现。
stop 和 begin 构成对照，讨论人类艺术家与计算机创造力的边界。
the work of the artist 与 the creativity of the computer 是并列对比对象。

sentences.sentence: Consider one of the oldest machine artists, Aaron, a robot that has had paintings exhibited in London's Tate Modern and the San Francisco Museum of Modern Art.
sentences.translation: 以最早的机器艺术家之一 Aaron 为例，它是一个机器人，其画作曾在伦敦泰特现代美术馆和旧金山现代艺术博物馆展出。

structure.text: Consider one of the oldest machine artists
a robot that has had paintings exhibited in London's Tate Modern and the San Francisco Museum of Modern Art
that has had paintings exhibited in London's Tate Modern and the San Francisco Museum of Modern Art

difficult_points: Consider 是祈使句，用来引出例子 Aaron。
Aaron 和 a robot... 都是对 one of the oldest machine artists 的同位解释。
has had paintings exhibited 是“have + 宾语 + 过去分词”结构，表示其画作被展出。

sentences.sentence: Aaron can pick up a paintbrush and paint on canvas on its own.
sentences.translation: Aaron 能够自己拿起画笔，在画布上作画。
difficult_points: pick up 和 paint 是并列动作，共用情态动词 can。
on its own 强调机器人可独立完成这些动作。

sentences.sentence: Impressive perhaps, but it is still little more than a tool to realise the programmer's own creative ideas.
sentences.translation: 这也许令人印象深刻，但它仍不过是实现程序员本人创意想法的工具。

structure.text: but it is still little more than a tool
to realise the programmer's own creative ideas

difficult_points: Impressive perhaps 是省略句，相当于 It is impressive perhaps。
little more than 表示“不过是”，削弱前面对 Aaron 能力的肯定。
to realise... 说明 tool 的用途。

sentences.sentence: Simon Colton, the designer of the Painting Fool, is keen to make sure his creation doesn't attract the same criticism.
sentences.translation: “绘画傻瓜”的设计者西蒙·科尔顿急于确保自己的作品不会招致同样的批评。

structure.text: the designer of the Painting Fool
his creation doesn't attract the same criticism

difficult_points: the designer of the Painting Fool 是同位语，补充 Simon Colton 的身份。
be keen to 表示“急切想要”，体现 Colton 对评价的重视。
make sure 后接省略 that 的宾语从句，说明要确保的内容。

sentences.sentence: Unlike earlier 'artists' such as Aaron, the Painting Fool only needs minimal direction and can come up with its own concepts by going online for material.
sentences.translation: 与 Aaron 这样的早期“艺术家”不同，“绘画傻瓜”只需要很少指导，并能通过上网寻找素材来形成自己的概念。

structure.text: and can come up with its own concepts

difficult_points: Unlike... 放在句首建立与 Aaron 的对比。
needs 和 can come up with 是并列谓语，说明 Painting Fool 的自主性更强。
by going online for material 说明形成概念的方式。

sentences.sentence: The software runs its own web searches and trawls through social media sites.
sentences.translation: 该软件会自行进行网络搜索，并浏览社交媒体网站搜寻内容。

structure.text: and trawls through social media sites

difficult_points: runs 和 trawls 是并列谓语，强调软件主动搜集素材。
trawls through 是比喻用法，指大量浏览、搜罗信息。

sentences.sentence: It is now beginning to display a kind of imagination too, creating pictures from scratch.
sentences.translation: 它现在也开始展现出某种想象力，能够从零开始创作图像。
difficult_points: It 指代 the Painting Fool 或该软件。
is beginning to display 表示能力正在逐步显现。
creating pictures from scratch 是现在分词短语，补充说明 imagination 的表现。

sentences.sentence: One of its original works is a series of fuzzy landscapes, depicting trees and sky.
sentences.translation: 它的一件原创作品是一组模糊的风景画，描绘树木和天空。
difficult_points: One of 后接复数 works，谓语用单数 is，因为主语核心是 One。
depicting... 是现在分词短语，后置说明 landscapes 的内容。
original works 强调这些作品不是直接复制已有图像。

sentences.sentence: While some might say they have a mechanical look, Colton argues that such reactions arise from people's double standards towards software-produced and human-produced art.
sentences.translation: 虽然有些人可能会说这些作品看起来机械化，但科尔顿认为，这类反应源自人们对软件创作艺术和人类创作艺术的双重标准。

structure.text: While some might say they have a mechanical look
that such reactions arise from people's double standards towards software-produced and human-produced art

difficult_points: While 引导让步从句，先承认反对者可能的评价。
they 指代前一句的 fuzzy landscapes。
that 从句说明 Colton 对这种评价来源的解释。
software-produced and human-produced art 是两个并列的被动合成形容词短语。

sentences.sentence: After all, he says, consider that the Painting Fool painted the landscapes without referring to a photo.
sentences.translation: 毕竟，他说，想想看，“绘画傻瓜”在没有参考照片的情况下画出了这些风景。

structure.text: that the Painting Fool painted the landscapes without referring to a photo

difficult_points: After all 引出 Colton 为机器创造力辩护的理由。
he says 是插入的引述语，不影响 consider 的祈使结构。
without referring to a photo 说明作品并非临摹照片。

sentences.sentence: 'If a child painted a new scene from its head, you'd say it has a certain level of imagination,' he points out.
sentences.translation: 他指出：“如果一个孩子凭脑海想象画出一个新场景，你会说他具有一定程度的想象力。”

structure.text: If a child painted a new scene from its head
it has a certain level of imagination

difficult_points: If 引导假设条件，用儿童绘画类比机器绘画。
you'd say 后接省略 that 的宾语从句。
from its head 表示凭脑海中的想象，而非照着实物或照片。

sentences.sentence: The same should be true of a machine.'
sentences.translation: 同样的判断也应适用于机器。”
difficult_points: The same 指上一句对孩子想象力的判断。
be true of 表示“适用于”，Colton 将儿童绘画的标准延伸到机器。

sentences.sentence: Software bugs can also lead to unexpected results.
sentences.translation: 软件漏洞也可能带来意想不到的结果。
difficult_points: can 表示可能性，而非必然结果。
also 表明这是除自主搜索和想象力之外，机器产生新效果的另一来源。

sentences.sentence: Some of the Painting Fool's paintings of a chair came out in black and white, thanks to a technical glitch.
sentences.translation: 由于一个技术故障，“绘画傻瓜”的一些椅子画作呈现为黑白效果。

structure.text: Some of the Painting Fool's paintings of a chair

difficult_points: came out 表示最终呈现出来的效果。
thanks to 在此说明原因，语气可中性或略带反讽。
technical glitch 指技术故障，而非艺术家有意选择。

sentences.sentence: This gives the work an eerie, ghostlike quality.
sentences.translation: 这赋予作品一种怪异、幽灵般的特质。
difficult_points: This 指上一句黑白故障产生的视觉效果。
give the work a quality 是双宾语结构，表示赋予作品某种特质。

sentences.sentence: Human artists like the renowned Ellsworth Kelly are lauded for limiting their colour palette - so why should computers be any different?
sentences.translation: 像著名的埃尔斯沃思·凯利这样的人类艺术家会因限制自己的调色板而受到赞扬——那么为什么计算机就应当有所不同呢？

structure.text: Human artists like the renowned Ellsworth Kelly
are lauded for limiting their colour palette
so why should computers be any different

difficult_points: are lauded 是被动语态，表示人类艺术家因某种限制性选择而被赞扬。
for limiting... 说明被赞扬的原因。
so why should computers be any different 是反问，指出不应对计算机艺术采用不同标准。

sentences.sentence: Researchers like Colton don't believe it is right to measure machine creativity directly to that of humans who 'have had millennia to develop our skills'.
sentences.translation: 像科尔顿这样的研究者并不认为，把机器创造力直接拿来和已经“有数千年时间发展技能”的人类创造力衡量比较是正确的。

structure.text: it is right to measure machine creativity directly to that of humans who 'have had millennia to develop our skills'
to measure machine creativity directly to that of humans
who 'have had millennia to develop our skills'

difficult_points: believe 后省略 that，引导宾语从句 it is right...。
it 是形式主语，真正主语是不定式 to measure...。
that of humans 中 that 指代 creativity，避免重复。
who 引导定语从句修饰 humans，引号中是研究者对人类长期发展的强调。

sentences.sentence: Others, though, are fascinated by the prospect that a computer might create something as original and subtle as our best artists.
sentences.translation: 不过，另一些人则着迷于这样一种前景：计算机也许能创造出像我们最优秀艺术家的作品一样原创而精妙的东西。

structure.text: the prospect that a computer might create something as original and subtle as our best artists
that a computer might create something as original and subtle as our best artists

difficult_points: though 是插入副词，表示与前一句研究者态度形成对比。
the prospect 后接 that 同位语从句，解释令人着迷的前景是什么。
as original and subtle as... 是同级比较，比较计算机作品与最佳艺术家的作品。

sentences.sentence: So far, only one has come close.
sentences.translation: 到目前为止，只有一个程序接近做到这一点。
difficult_points: So far 与现在完成时 has come close 搭配，表示截至目前的结果。
only one 指代能够接近创造出高度原创细腻作品的计算机程序。

sentences.sentence: Composer David Cope invented a program called Experiments in Musical Intelligence, or EMI.
sentences.translation: 作曲家大卫·科普发明了一个名为“音乐智能实验”的程序，简称 EMI。

structure.text: a program called Experiments in Musical Intelligence

difficult_points: Composer David Cope 中 Composer 是身份名词，修饰人名。
called... 是过去分词短语，后置说明 program 的名称。
or EMI 给出该程序的简称。

sentences.sentence: Not only did EMI create compositions in Cope's style, but also that of the most revered classical composers, including Bach, Chopin and Mozart.
sentences.translation: EMI 不仅能创作科普风格的作品，还能创作最受推崇的古典作曲家的风格作品，包括巴赫、肖邦和莫扎特。

structure.text: Not only did EMI create compositions in Cope's style
but also that of the most revered classical composers

difficult_points: Not only 置于句首引发部分倒装，所以使用 did EMI create。
but also that of... 中 that 指代 compositions/style 类内容，表达省略。
including... 列举 most revered classical composers 的代表。

sentences.sentence: Audiences were moved to tears, and EMI even fooled classical music experts into thinking they were hearing genuine Bach.
sentences.translation: 观众被感动得落泪，EMI 甚至骗过古典音乐专家，让他们以为自己听到的是真正的巴赫作品。

structure.text: EMI even fooled classical music experts into thinking they were hearing genuine Bach
thinking they were hearing genuine Bach

difficult_points: were moved to tears 是被动语态，表示观众被音乐感动到哭。
fooled...into thinking 是固定结构，表示欺骗某人使其相信。
they were hearing genuine Bach 是 thinking 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: Not everyone was impressed however.
sentences.translation: 然而，并不是所有人都对此印象深刻。
difficult_points: Not everyone 是部分否定，表示“并非所有人”。
however 放在句尾，标记与前面观众和专家被骗过的积极反应形成转折。

sentences.sentence: Some, such as Wiggins, have blasted Cope's work as pseudoscience, and condemned him for his deliberately vague explanation of how the software worked.
sentences.translation: 有些人，比如威金斯，猛烈抨击科普的工作是伪科学，并谴责他对软件运作方式作出故意含糊的解释。

structure.text: have blasted Cope's work as pseudoscience
and condemned him for his deliberately vague explanation of how the software worked

difficult_points: such as Wiggins 是插入例子，说明 Some 包括哪些批评者。
have blasted 和 condemned 是并列谓语，分别表示批评作品与谴责作者。
how the software worked 是 explanation of 的宾语内容，说明解释的对象。

sentences.sentence: Meanwhile, Douglas Hofstadter of Indiana University said EMI created replicas which still rely completely on the original artist's creative impulses.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，印第安纳大学的道格拉斯·霍夫施塔特说，EMI 创造的是复制品，仍然完全依赖原艺术家的创造冲动。

structure.text: EMI created replicas which still rely completely on the original artist's creative impulses
which still rely completely on the original artist's creative impulses

difficult_points: said 后省略 that，引导宾语从句。
which 引导定语从句修饰 replicas，说明这些复制品仍依赖原艺术家。
creative impulses 指原艺术家的创作冲动或灵感来源。

sentences.sentence: When audiences found out the truth they were often outraged with Cope, and one music lover even tried to punch him.
sentences.translation: 当观众得知真相后，他们常常对科普感到愤怒，甚至有一位音乐爱好者试图打他。

structure.text: When audiences found out the truth
they were often outraged with Cope
and one music lover even tried to punch him

difficult_points: When 引导时间状语从句，说明愤怒发生在观众得知真相之后。
they 指代 audiences。
outraged with Cope 表示对 Cope 本人愤怒，而非只是不喜欢音乐。

sentences.sentence: Amid such controversy, Cope destroyed EMI's vital databases.
sentences.translation: 在这样的争议中，科普销毁了 EMI 的关键数据库。
difficult_points: Amid such controversy 表示在巨大争议背景下发生的动作。
vital databases 指 EMI 的核心数据库，销毁它意味着程序基础被破坏。

sentences.sentence: But why did so many people love the music, yet recoil when they discovered how it was composed?
sentences.translation: 但是，为什么这么多人喜爱这些音乐，却在发现它们是如何创作出来时退缩反感呢？

structure.text: why did so many people love the music
when they discovered how it was composed

difficult_points: But 引出对上一段争议的追问，推动文章进入心理原因分析。
love 与 recoil 形成强烈转折，说明评价随创作来源认知改变。
how it was composed 是 discovered 的宾语从句。

sentences.sentence: A study by computer scientist David Moffat of Glasgow Caledonian University provides a clue.
sentences.translation: 格拉斯哥卡利多尼安大学计算机科学家大卫·莫法特的一项研究提供了线索。

structure.text: A study by computer scientist David Moffat of Glasgow Caledonian University

difficult_points: 主语核心是 A study，后面 by...of... 说明研究者身份和所属大学。
provides a clue 表示该研究并非直接给出完整答案，而是提供解释线索。

sentences.sentence: He asked both expert musicians and non-experts to assess six compositions.
sentences.translation: 他请专业音乐家和非专业人士都来评估六首作品。
difficult_points: both...and... 连接两类受试者，强调研究覆盖专家和非专家。
ask sb to do sth 结构中 to assess... 是宾语补足语。

sentences.sentence: The participants weren't told beforehand whether the tunes were composed by humans or computers, but were asked to guess, and then rate how much they liked each one.
sentences.translation: 参与者事先没有被告知这些曲调是由人类还是计算机创作的，而是被要求先猜测，然后评价自己对每一首的喜欢程度。

structure.text: whether the tunes were composed by humans or computers
and then rate how much they liked each one
how much they liked each one

difficult_points: weren't told 与 were asked 共用主语 The participants，都是被动语态。
whether...or... 引导宾语从句，说明没有提前告知的内容。
rate 后接 how much 引导的宾语从句，表示评分对象是喜欢程度。

sentences.sentence: People who thought the composer was a computer tended to dislike the piece more than those who believed it was human.
sentences.translation: 认为作曲者是计算机的人，往往比那些认为作曲者是人类的人更不喜欢这首作品。

structure.text: People who thought the composer was a computer
who thought the composer was a computer
the piece more than those who believed it was human

difficult_points: 两个 who 从句分别修饰 People 和 those，比较两类判断不同的人。
tended to 表示倾向，而非每个个体必然如此。
more than those... 构成比较，说明认为是计算机创作会降低喜爱程度。

sentences.sentence: This was true even among the experts, who might have been expected to be more objective in their analyses.
sentences.translation: 即使在专家中也是如此，而人们本来可能期待他们在分析中更加客观。

structure.text: This was true even among the experts
who might have been expected to be more objective in their analyses
to be more objective in their analyses

difficult_points: This 指上一句“认为是计算机创作会更不喜欢作品”的现象。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 experts 的预期特征。
might have been expected to 是情态动词完成式的被动结构，表示本来可能被期待。

sentences.sentence: Where does this prejudice come from?
sentences.translation: 这种偏见来自哪里？
difficult_points: this prejudice 指上一段中人们因作品被认为是计算机创作而降低评价的偏见。
come from 询问来源，引出后文关于艺术体验心理机制的解释。

sentences.sentence: Paul Bloom of Yale University has a suggestion: he reckons part of the pleasure we get from art stems from the creative process behind the work.
sentences.translation: 耶鲁大学的保罗·布鲁姆提出了一个解释：他认为，我们从艺术中获得的部分愉悦来自作品背后的创作过程。

structure.text: part of the pleasure we get from art stems from the creative process behind the work

difficult_points: 冒号后解释 Paul Bloom 的 suggestion 具体是什么。
reckons 后省略 that，引出观点内容。
we get from art 是省略关系词的定语从句，修饰 pleasure。
stems from 表示愉悦的一部分来源于创作过程。

sentences.sentence: This can give it an 'irresistible essence', says Bloom.
sentences.translation: 布鲁姆说，这能赋予它一种“不可抗拒的本质”。
difficult_points: This 指前一句所说的创作过程背后的意义。
give it an essence 是双宾语结构，it 指艺术作品。
says Bloom 是后置引述语，说明观点来源。

sentences.sentence: Meanwhile, experiments by Justin Kruger of New York University have shown that people's enjoyment of an artwork increases if they think more time and effort was needed to create it.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，纽约大学贾斯廷·克鲁格的实验表明，如果人们认为创作一件艺术品需要更多时间和努力，他们对这件作品的享受程度就会提高。

structure.text: experiments by Justin Kruger of New York University
that people's enjoyment of an artwork increases if they think more time and effort was needed to create it
if they think more time and effort was needed to create it

difficult_points: have shown 后接 that 宾语从句，说明实验结论。
if 从句嵌在 that 从句内部，说明 enjoyment increases 的条件。
was needed to create it 是被动结构，it 指 artwork。

expressions.meaning_zh: 人们对艺术品的享受程度

sentences.sentence: Similarly, Colton thinks that when people experience art, they wonder what the artist might have been thinking or what the artist is trying to tell them.
sentences.translation: 类似地，科尔顿认为，当人们体验艺术时，他们会猜想艺术家当时可能在想什么，或者艺术家试图向他们传达什么。

structure.text: that when people experience art, they wonder what the artist might have been thinking or what the artist is trying to tell them
what the artist might have been thinking
what the artist is trying to tell them

difficult_points: that 引导 thinks 的宾语从句，内部又含 when 时间状语从句。
两个 what 从句并列，都是 wonder 的宾语。
might have been thinking 是情态动词完成进行式，表示对艺术家过去心理状态的推测。

sentences.sentence: It seems obvious, therefore, that with computers producing art, this speculation is cut short - there's nothing to explore.
sentences.translation: 因此，显而易见的是，当计算机创作艺术时，这种推测被截断了——没有什么可探索的。

structure.text: that with computers producing art, this speculation is cut short

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正内容是 that 从句。
with computers producing art 是 with 复合结构，表示背景条件。
is cut short 是被动语态，说明关于艺术家意图的推测被中断。
破折号后 there's nothing to explore 解释为什么推测被截断。

sentences.sentence: But as technology becomes increasingly complex, finding those greater depths in computer art could become possible.
sentences.translation: 但随着技术变得越来越复杂，在计算机艺术中发现那些更深层次的内涵可能会成为可能。

structure.text: finding those greater depths in computer art

difficult_points: as 引导时间/伴随状语从句，表示随着技术发展。
finding... 是动名词短语作主语。
could become possible 表示未来可能性，而非已经实现。

sentences.sentence: This is precisely why Colton asks the Painting Fool to tap into online social networks for its inspiration: hopefully this way it will choose themes that will already be meaningful to us.
sentences.translation: 这正是科尔顿让“绘画傻瓜”利用线上社交网络获取灵感的原因：希望通过这种方式，它会选择那些对我们来说已经有意义的主题。

structure.text: This is precisely why Colton asks the Painting Fool to tap into online social networks for its inspiration
why Colton asks the Painting Fool to tap into online social networks for its inspiration
to tap into online social networks for its inspiration
hopefully this way it will choose themes that will already be meaningful to us
that will already be meaningful to us

difficult_points: This 指上一句关于计算机艺术未来可能具有更深内涵的观点。
precisely why 强调 Colton 采取该做法的确切原因。
ask...to tap into... 是“要求/让某对象做某事”的结构。
冒号后解释这种做法的预期效果，that 从句修饰 themes。
